title: Asia Centric: Why Global Capital Is Pouring Into Japan Stocks
summary: What’s driving Japan’s historic bull market? And why are global investors finally returning to Tokyo after years on the sidelines? According to Goldman Sachs, it’s a rare convergence of factors including the global AI trade, inflation, corporate governance reform and an evolving macroeconomic landscape.

title: Electric Bike Rental Firm Lime Up 4% After $174 Million IPO
summary: The operator of Lime electric scooters and bikes gained 4% after the company and some of its shareholders raised $174 million in an initial public offering.

title: Stocks Fall on Korean Chip Selloff, Oil Declines: Markets Wrap
summary: Equities fell as a selloff in chip stocks spread to South Korea, reviving concerns the blistering rally in artificial intelligence shares this year may have gone too far, too fast. Brent slipped to the lowest since before the start of the Middle East war.

title: Carlsberg Said to File for India IPO to Raise $700 Million
summary: Carlsberg A/S has confidentially filed draft papers for an initial public offering of its India unit to raise as much as $700 million, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: FTSE 100 Set For Second Day of Declines
ECB Views Splinter on Next Rate Move as Inflation Sinks With Oil
summary: Just three weeks after the European Central Bank unanimously raised interest rates in response to the Iran war, there’s no consensus on what to do next.

title: Yen Edges Higher With Traders on Alert for Risk of Intervention
summary: The yen strengthened slightly against the dollar Thursday amid speculation that further intervention by Japan to bolster the currency may come without warning from officials.

title: A Dual-Currency Stock Market Is Congo’s Latest Bid to Entice Investors
summary: The Democratic Republic of Congo is drawing up plans for its first stock exchange as it seeks to attract more investment to an economy that’s benefiting from soaring demand for minerals critical to the AI buildout.

title: Palm Oil Dips on Weaker Crude and Malaysian Production Outlook
summary: Palm oil edged lower, weighed down by declining crude prices and expectations for strong output from the second-biggest grower, Malaysia.

title: Trump Hails Qatar Meetings on Iran as Talks to Continue
summary: US President Donald Trump said negotiators had made progress in indirect talks with Iran to move past last week’s tit-for-tat strikes and convert their interim truce into a lasting peace. Bloomberg's Stuart Livingstone-Wallace has the latest updates. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Japanese Bonds Fall as Inflation Concerns Curb Demand at Auction
summary: Japanese bonds dropped after demand at a sale of benchmark 10-year notes fell to the lowest since April on growing fiscal concerns and worries the Bank of Japan isn’t raising rates quickly enough to contain inflation.

title: India’s Textile Stocks Become Market Standouts on Trade Deals
summary: Indian suppliers of T-shirts, bed linen and towels to global retailers such as Walmart Inc. are among this year’s biggest stock-market winners, with some investors betting the rally has further to run.

title: US Tech Rout Sours Mood For Japan, South Korean Chip Stocks
summary: Japanese and South Korean semiconductor stocks tumbled Thursday after a selloff in US technology shares soured the mood across Asia. Meta Platforms Inc.’s plan to sell access to AI computing power also raised worries about oversupply.

title: KKR-Backed Musinsa Ramps Up Asia Store Push Ahead of IPO
summary: Musinsa Co., the KKR & Co.-backed South Korean fashion retailer, is rolling out an aggressive physical store expansion in China and Japan as it seeks to challenge Uniqlo and build momentum ahead of a closely watched initial public offering.

title: Perpetual Rejects $1.7 Billion Takeover Bid From PE Firm EQT
summary: Perpetual Ltd. has rejected a takeover offer from a company indirectly controlled by Swedish private equity firm EQT AB, the latest in a string of refusals to various suitors by the Australian asset manager.

title: Asia Deals Top $750 Billion Despite Mayhem in Parts of the World
summary: The world has faced plenty of disruption this year, but that hasn’t derailed business in and out of the Asia Pacific, where the volume of deals such as mergers and acquisitions has already topped $750 billion in 2026.

title: Foreign Traders Sell Biggest Pile of Japan Bonds in Three Years
summary: Overseas investors offloaded the most Japanese bonds in three years in June, official data showed, after a lackluster performance left the debt trailing most of its global peers.

title: Oil Extends Drop as More Barrels Flow Through Strait of Hormuz
summary: Oil fell for a third day as flows through the Strait of Hormuz climbed and there were signs of progress in indirect talks between the US and Iran.

title: Warsh Must Beware of Curves Flattening to Deceive
summary: The bond market is taking him seriously, but that’s not necessarily great news.

title: Business Jet Departures in Middle East Drop 30% During Iran War
summary: Business jet departures in the Middle East declined about 30% in the aftermath of the Iran war, a trend that’s at risk of continuing until a peace deal is signed.

title: Headline's Zhu on Asia Tech Opportunities
summary: Clarey Zhu, Partner at Headline Growth, discusses the investment opportunities in Asia's tech sector, including AI infrastructure, deep tech and robotics. She speaks with Haidi Stroud-Watts from the sidelines of the IVS2026 conference in Kyoto. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Tech Selloff Extends, Apple Seeks Chinese Chips | The Asia Trade 7/2/2026
summary: "Bloomberg: The Asia Trade" brings you everything you need to know to get ahead as the trading day begins in Asia. Bloomberg TV is live from Tokyo and Sydney with Shery Ahn and Haidi Stroud-Watts, getting insight and analysis from newsmakers and industry leaders on the biggest stories shaping global markets. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Citi's Luet: China's Franchise Has Grown Very Strongly
summary: Marc Luet, Head of Japan, Asia North & Australia and Banking at Citi, says the bank has seen the role of the yuan grow and is hopeful of obtaining a securities license in China. He speaks exclusively on "Bloomberg: The China Show." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Iran’s Floating Oil Stockpile Swells as Major Buyers Stay Away
summary: A hoard of Iranian oil is building up at sea, as the Islamic Republic struggles to find buyers before the expiry of a 60-day window granted by Washington.

title: Korean Stocks Fall as Jitters Over Meta and Apple Hit Chipmakers
summary: South Korean stocks slumped as Meta Platforms Inc.’s plan to sell computing power raised questions over excess in AI capacity, driving a selloff in chipmakers.

title: CATL Says Mining, Not Refining, Is Battery Making’s Big Hurdle
summary: Securing supplies of key raw materials has become a priority for the world’s largest manufacturer of electric vehicle batteries.

title: Iron Ore Advances as CMRG May Block Some Fortescue Deliveries
summary: Iron ore advanced — with futures briefly topping $100 a ton — after China’s state-backed buyer of the commodity signaled that it planned to restrict some Fortescue Ltd. inventories held at mainland ports.

title: Global Appetite for Korea Grows Ahead of 24-Hour Won Trading
summary: Global investors’ interest in South Korea’s economy and capital markets has “clearly changed” for the better in recent years, the country’s top finance ministry official said, as the won prepares to shift to round-the-clock trading on July 6.

title: Traders Plot Worst-Case Scenario for Yen If Crisis Hits
summary: The yen sliding to a once-unthinkable 200 per dollar level is now a medium-term risk — albeit an extreme one — for some investors.

title: Gold Extends Gain After Warsh Remarks Ease Rate-Hike Prospects
summary: Gold extended a rebound after a speech by US Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh dampened speculation the central bank may hike interest rates this year to tackle inflation.
